name: debug-value-editor
description: This extension allows to edit and view values of a debugee with a full text editor. Currently only supports javascript debug adaptors.

languageModelTools.name: listDebugSessions
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all active debug sessions in a hierarchy. Returns IDs (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss), names, expression language IDs, and active status. Note: Many more debugging tools (evaluate, step, inspect variables, etc.) become available once you start a debug session using launchNodeRepl, launchDenoRepl, launchNodeProgram, or startDebugSession.

languageModelTools.name: startDebugSession
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Start a new debug session with an arbitrary debug configuration. Supports any debug type (pwa-node, pwa-chrome, python, etc.) with full configuration options. Returns the debug session ID. Once started, additional tools for evaluation, stepping, inspection, and more become available.

configuration.description: The VS Code debug configuration object. Must include 'type' (e.g., 'pwa-node', 'pwa-chrome', 'python') and 'request' ('launch' or 'attach'). Other properties depend on the debug type. Common properties: 'name' (session name), 'program' (file to debug), 'cwd' (working directory), 'args' (command-line arguments), 'url' (for browser debugging), 'port' (for attach).

request.description: Whether to launch a new process or attach to an existing one

name.description: A name for the debug session (optional, will be auto-generated if not provided)

languageModelTools.name: launchNodeProgram
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Launch a new Node.js debug session for a JavaScript/TypeScript file. Returns the debug session ID. Once started, additional tools for evaluation, stepping, inspection, and more become available.

program.description: The absolute path to the JavaScript or TypeScript file to debug

args.description: Optional command-line arguments to pass to the program

stopOnEntry.description: Whether to stop execution at the first line of the program (default: false)

cwd.description: The working directory for the program

languageModelTools.name: launchNodeRepl
languageModelTools.modelDescription: PREFERRED: Launch an interactive Node.js REPL in debug mode. Use this instead of running scripts via terminal when you need to quickly evaluate JavaScript/TypeScript code - no temporary files needed! Just launch the REPL and use evaluateExpressionInDebugSession to run code. Perfect for quick calculations, testing code snippets, exploring APIs, or prototyping. The session persists state between evaluations. Returns the debug session ID. Once started, evaluation and many other debugging tools become available.

cwd.description: The working directory for the REPL

languageModelTools.name: stopDebugSession
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Stop a running debug session.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session to stop (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

languageModelTools.name: restartDebugSession
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Restart a debug session with the same configuration. Returns the new debug session ID.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session to restart (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

languageModelTools.name: evaluateExpressionInDebugSession
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Evaluate an expression in a specific debug session. If evaluation takes longer than timeoutMs, returns a requestId that can be used with getEvaluationResult to check the result later.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

expression.description: The expression to evaluate in the debug session

timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds (default: 1000). If evaluation takes longer, returns a requestId for later retrieval.

languageModelTools.name: getEvaluationResult
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the result of a pending evaluation by its requestId. Use this after evaluateExpressionInDebugSession times out.

requestId.description: The requestId returned from a timed-out evaluation

timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds (default: 1000). If still pending, returns the same requestId.

languageModelTools.name: stepInto
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Step into the next function call. Returns the new location with source code context (3 lines above and below).

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

languageModelTools.name: stepOver
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Step over the current line. Returns the new location with source code context (3 lines above and below).

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

languageModelTools.name: stepOut
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Step out of the current function. Returns the new location with source code context (3 lines above and below).

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

languageModelTools.name: runToLine
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Continue execution until reaching the specified line. Sets a temporary breakpoint and continues. Returns the stop location with source code context.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

filePath.description: The absolute path to the source file

line.description: The line number to run to (1-indexed)

languageModelTools.name: getStackTrace
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the call stack of the paused debug session. Returns up to maxFrames frames with the top frame including source code context. If there are more frames, middle frames are skipped.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

maxFrames.description: Maximum number of frames to return (default: 10). Top frames get priority, middle frames are skipped if needed.

languageModelTools.name: getLocalVariables
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get local variables in the current stack frame of a paused debug session.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

maxVariables.description: Maximum number of variables to return (default: 20). Long values are truncated.

languageModelTools.name: getLineContext
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get source code context around a specific line. Returns 3 lines above, the target line (marked with >), and 3 lines below with line numbers.

filePath.description: The absolute path to the source file

languageModelTools.name: setBreakpoint
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set a breakpoint at a specific line in a source file. Returns the actual line where the breakpoint was set (may differ from requested) and the source code context.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss)

filePath.description: The absolute path to the source file

line.description: The line number where to set the breakpoint (1-indexed)

languageModelTools.name: captureDomNodeScreenshot
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Capture a screenshot of a DOM element in a browser debug session. The expression should evaluate to a DOM element (e.g., document.querySelector('.my-class') or a variable holding a DOM element). Returns the screenshot as an image along with element information. Only works with browser/chrome debug sessions.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss). Must be a browser/chrome debug session.

expression.description: A JavaScript expression that evaluates to a DOM element (e.g., 'document.querySelector("#myElement")', 'document.body', or a variable name holding a DOM element)

languageModelTools.name: getLoadedScripts
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all scripts loaded in a JS debug session. Supports filtering by regex pattern and pagination. Returns script IDs (needed for getScriptSource), URLs, line counts, and source map info. Max ~40KB output, will truncate if exceeded.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss). Must be a JS debug session.

searchPattern.description: Optional regex pattern to filter scripts by URL (case-insensitive)

limit.description: Maximum number of scripts to return (default: 50)

languageModelTools.name: getScriptSource
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Get the source code of a loaded script with line numbers. Use getLoadedScripts first to get script IDs. Supports two modes: (1) Without searchPattern: returns lines with pagination. (2) With searchPattern: returns only matching lines with ±contextLines of context. Max ~40KB output, will truncate if exceeded.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss). Must be a JS debug session.

searchPattern.description: Optional regex pattern to search within the script. If provided, returns only matching lines with context.

offset.description: Starting line (0-indexed, without searchPattern) or starting match index (with searchPattern). Default: 0

limit.description: Maximum lines to return (without searchPattern) or max matches to return (with searchPattern). Default: 100

contextLines.description: Lines of context around each match when using searchPattern (default: 5)

languageModelTools.name: launchDenoRepl
languageModelTools.modelDescription: PREFERRED for npm packages: Launch a Deno REPL in debug mode. Deno auto-fetches npm packages without manual installation! Use 'await import("npm:package-name")' to import any npm package. Perfect for quick experiments with external packages. Example: const _ = await import('npm:lodash'). Requires Deno to be installed. Returns the debug session ID. Once started, evaluation and many other debugging tools become available.

cwd.description: The working directory for the REPL

languageModelTools.name: cdpRequest
languageModelTools.modelDescription: WARNING: Low-level tool - only use if other tools don't provide the needed functionality! Send a raw Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) request. This gives direct access to CDP methods like Runtime.*, Debugger.*, DOM.*, Network.*, etc. Refer to https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/ for available methods. Only works with JS debug sessions.

debugSessionId.description: The ID of the debug session (format: id:N-time:DDThh:mm:ss). Must be a JS debug session.

params.description: The parameters to pass to the CDP method (method-specific)

scripts.build: npm run build-ts && npm run build-vsix
